problem.title: Remove Linked List Elements
problem.description: Given the head of a linked list and an integer val, remove all the nodes of the linked list that has Node.val == val, and return the new head.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Two Pointers
solutions.description: Use a dummy head node to eliminate edge cases when the target value appears at the head. Maintain a prev pointer and a curr pointer; when curr's value matches val, bypass the node by linking prev.next to curr.next; otherwise advance prev to curr.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import Optional

# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next

def removeElements(head: Optional[object], val: int) -> Optional[object]:
    dummy = type('ListNode', (), {'val': 0, 'next': head})()
    prev, curr = dummy, head

    while curr:
        if curr.val == val:
            prev.next = curr.next
        else:
            prev = curr
        curr = curr.next

    return dummy.next
